query: Nigel Farage Clacton 2024 general election vote share percentage and votes, turnout, and Reform UK national polling 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Farage's Clacton 2024 result:**
- **46.2% vote share (21,225 votes)** — a Reform UK gain from the Conservatives
- **Majority: 8,405 votes (18.3%)** over Conservative Giles Watling (27.9%); Labour third at 16.2%
- **Turnout: 58.7%** (45,958 valid votes from an 78,245 electorate)
- Won on a plurality (below 50%) under First Past the Post

**Reform UK 2026 national polling:**
- Reform now **leads national polls in the mid-to-high 20s** (YouGov 5–6 July 2026: 25%; peaked at 27% in late May 2026), up from ~14.3% at the 2024 GE
- Won ~26–27% national vote share in the 7 May 2026 local elections
- Political landscape is highly fragmented; Labour collapsed, triggering Starmer's resignation

**Critical by-election context:** Farage resigned as Clacton MP on **7 July 2026** amid a Reform UK donations scandal, triggering a by-election he will contest — and reporting indicates **other major parties said they would not field candidates**, which is highly relevant to by-election vote-share expectations.

I've delivered the full structured findings via the StructuredOutput tool.
